text_blocks.content:
Client Summary Report Preparation – Execution Document
Part 1 – Ownership & Execution Plan
1. Initial Approach
Upon receiving the dataset, I would begin with a structured data audit before attempting any
reporting. The dataset contains 20 transactions with the following fields: Transaction_ID, Date,
Customer, Packages_Delivered, Packages_Paid, Delivery_Source, and Finance_Source.
My first priority would be:
1. Validate data integrity (missing values, duplicates, type mismatches).
2. Compare operational vs financial metrics (Delivered vs Paid).
3. Identify inconsistencies across systems.
Initial findings show discrepancies between Packages_Delivered and Packages_Paid in multiple
transactions. This becomes the primary analytical focus since it directly affects financial
accuracy.
Workflow Structure
1. Data Profiling
o Check nulls, duplicates, field types
o Validate unique Transaction_IDs
2. Reconciliation Analysis
o Create a calculated field: Difference = Paid − Delivered
o Categorize: Overpayment, Underpayment, Match
3. Pattern Analysis
o Assess frequency and magnitude of discrepancies
o Identify high-risk outliers
4. Reporting Structure Draft
o Executive Summary
o Key Metrics
o Reconciliation Findings
o Risk & Recommendations
text_blocks.section_hint: Client Summary Report Preparation – Execution Document

text_blocks.content:
2. Handling Ambiguity
Assumptions Made
 Packages_Delivered reflects operational truth (System A).
 Packages_Paid reflects financial record (System B).
 All transactions belong to the same reporting period.
What I Would Clarify
 Which system is the source of truth?
 Should discrepancies be flagged or adjusted?
 Is this report operational, financial, or executive-facing?
How I Avoid Incorrect Conclusions
 Clearly label assumptions in the report.
 Separate confirmed findings from interpretative insights.
 Quantify discrepancies rather than speculate causes.
3. Use of AI
AI for Data Analysis
Example prompts:
 “Identify inconsistencies between delivered and paid quantities and summarize patterns.”
 “Detect statistical outliers in transaction differences.”
AI for Structuring Report
 “Create an executive-level summary of reconciliation findings.”
 “Rewrite findings in a client-ready tone.”
AI for Risk Detection
 “Based on these discrepancies, identify potential operational or financial risks.”
AI accelerates summarization and structuring but does not replace validation.

4. Risk Identification
Risk Impact Mitigation
Financial discrepancies Revenue leakage or overbilling Quantify total variance
Undefined reporting format Client dissatisfaction Draft executive-friendly structure
Incorrect assumptions Loss of credibility Explicitly document assumptions
5. Progress Strategy
To avoid getting blocked:
 Move forward with documented assumptions.
 Create a draft report while awaiting clarification.
 Quantify all findings before escalation.
 Prepare specific questions instead of general concerns.
This ensures progress continues despite uncertainty.
Part 2 – Blocker Analysis & Escalation
Blocker 1
Blocker Description: No defined source of truth between Delivery and Finance systems.
Why It Matters: Impacts which numbers are considered accurate.
What I Tried: Performed reconciliation and quantified differences.
Proposed Solution: Confirm system authority before finalizing conclusions.
Blocker 2
Blocker Description: Report format undefined (technical vs executive).
Why It Matters: Misaligned output could reduce client value.
What I Tried: Drafted a neutral executive-style summary.
Proposed Solution: Confirm audience level and preferred format.
Blocker 3
Blocker Description: Root cause of discrepancies unknown.
Why It Matters: Cannot recommend corrective action confidently.

What I Tried: Identified magnitude and frequency of mismatches.
Proposed Solution: Request operational explanation for variance.
Escalation Message
Subject: Clarification on Data Reconciliation for Client Report
Hi [Manager Name],
I’ve completed an initial reconciliation of the dataset and identified multiple discrepancies
between Packages_Delivered (System A) and Packages_Paid (System B), including several
high-variance transactions. I have quantified the differences and structured a draft executive-
ready summary.
Before finalizing, I would like confirmation on which system should be treated as the source of
truth for reporting purposes. Additionally, could you confirm whether this report is intended for
executive stakeholders or operational teams?
In parallel, I will continue refining the reconciliation summary and risk section.
Please advise on the preferred direction so I can finalize confidently.
Best regards,
[Your Name]
Part 3 – AI-Assisted Problem Solving Reflection
AI Usage Strategy
I used AI primarily for structured thinking and summarization support. Prompts were analytical
and directive, such as identifying inconsistencies, summarizing reconciliation results, and
drafting executive-level summaries.
Evaluation of AI Outputs
Useful outputs:
 Pattern identification in discrepancies.
 Structuring of executive summaries.
 Risk framing suggestions.
Required modification:
text_blocks.section_hint: What I Tried: Identified magnitude and frequency of mismatches.

text_blocks.content:
 AI occasionally assumed causes without evidence.
 Some summaries lacked quantification.
Rejected:
 Any conclusions about operational failure without supporting data.
Verification & Judgment
All AI-generated insights were validated against the dataset. Calculations were independently
verified. I ensured that insights were data-backed and assumptions explicitly labeled.
Limitation of AI
AI does not inherently understand business context or system hierarchy. It may detect anomalies
but cannot determine organizational truth. I compensated by applying judgment, isolating factual
findings from interpretative insights, and preparing clarification questions for leadership.
text_blocks.section_hint:  AI occasionally assumed causes without evidence.
